from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a formatting lambda (subject, object) -> phrase
    # Helper to turn ISO dates into a readable form, e.g. 1996-05-30 -> 30 May 1996
    def _format_date(date_str: str) -> str:
        try:
            from datetime import datetime
            dt = datetime.strptime(date_str, "%Y-%m-%d")
            return dt.strftime("%-d %B %Y")
        except Exception:
            return date_str

    predicate_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {_format_date(o)}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays in the {o}" if any(tok in o.lower() for tok in ["division", "league", "premier", "championship", "liga", "serie"]) else f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} previously played for {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the manager of {s}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the coach of {s}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {o}th pick",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft team is {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} played for youth club {o}",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made his debut with {o}",
        # Additional predicates beyond the athlete domain
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{o} owns {s}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the chairman of {s}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the general manager of {s}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an ethnic group of {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language in {s} is {o.replace(' language', '')}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a UTC offset of {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {o} time zone",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to build a compact description per entity
    subject_map = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    def join_parts(parts):
        """Join a list of strings with commas and an 'and' before the last element."""
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        return ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]

    sentences = []
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        # Preserve the order of appearance for readability
        phrase_parts = []
        # Handle predicates that may have multiple objects (e.g., club, currentclub)
        multi_obj_preds = {"club", "currentclub"}
        # Collect objects for multi‑object predicates first
        multi_objs = {p: [] for p in multi_obj_preds}
        # Collect location information (city, country) to build a single location phrase
        location_info = {}
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            if pred in {"city", "country"}:
                location_info[pred] = obj
                continue
            if pred in multi_obj_preds:
                multi_objs[pred].append(obj)
            else:
                # Basic phrase for the predicate
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)

                # If the object is also a subject elsewhere, add a relative clause with its simple descriptors
                if obj in subject_map:
                    # Collect simple descriptors of the linked entity for a relative clause
                    linked_parts = []
                    # predicates that make sense in a relative clause
                    rel_predicates = {
                        "manager": lambda o: f"manager is {o}",
                        "generalManager": lambda o: f"general manager is {o}",
                        "coach": lambda o: f"coach is {o}",
                        "owner": lambda o: f"owner is {o}",
                        "chairman": lambda o: f"chairman is {o}",
                        "ground": lambda o: f"ground is {o}",
                        "stadium": lambda o: f"stadium is {o}",
                        "city": lambda o: f"city is {o}",
                        "country": lambda o: f"country is {o}",
                        "league": lambda o: f"league is {o}",
                        "birthPlace": lambda o: f"birth place is {o}",
                        "birthDate": lambda o: f"birth date is {_format_date(o)}",
                        "birthYear": lambda o: f"born in {o}",
                        "language": lambda o: f"language is {o.replace(' language', '')}",
                        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"official language is {o}",
                        "currency": lambda o: f"currency is {o}",
                        "demonym": lambda o: f"demonym is {o}",
                    }
                    for p2, o2 in subject_map[obj]:
                        if p2 in rel_predicates:
                            linked_parts.append(rel_predicates[p2](o2))
                    if linked_parts:
                        clause = ", ".join(linked_parts)
                        phrase += f", where {clause}"

                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Add a combined location phrase if city/country information is present
        if location_info:
            city = location_info.get("city")
            country = location_info.get("country")
            if city and country:
                loc_phrase = f"Based in {city}, {country}, the {subj}"
            elif city:
                loc_phrase = f"Based in {city}, the {subj}"
            else:
                loc_phrase = f"The {subj} is in {country}"
            # Insert at the beginning of phrase_parts
            phrase_parts.insert(0, loc_phrase)

        # Add combined phrases for multi‑object predicates, with special handling for clubs to attach manager info
        for pred, objs in multi_objs.items():
            if not objs:
                continue
            # Build a readable list of objects
            if len(objs) > 1:
                objs_str = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + f", and {objs[-1]}"
            else:
                objs_str = objs[0]

            # Basic phrase
            phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, objs_str)

            # If the predicate is club (or currentclub) and any of the clubs have a manager, add a relative clause
            if pred in {"club", "currentclub"}:
                manager_clauses = []
                for club_obj in objs:
                    if club_obj in subject_map:
                        for p2, o2 in subject_map[club_obj]:
                            if p2 == "manager":
                                manager_clauses.append(o2)
                if manager_clauses:
                    # If only one manager, use singular "which is managed by"
                    if len(manager_clauses) == 1:
                        phrase += f", which is managed by {manager_clauses[0]}"
                    else:
                        mgrs = ", ".join(manager_clauses[:-1]) + f", and {manager_clauses[-1]}"
                        phrase += f", which are managed by {mgrs}"
            phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Join all predicate phrases for this subject into one clause
        subject_clause = join_parts(phrase_parts)
        sentences.append(subject_clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(sentences) + "."
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

                        for p2, o2 in subject_map[club_obj]:

                                manager_clauses.append(o2)
                if manager_clauses:
                    # If only one manager, use singular "which is managed by"
                    if len(manager_clauses) == 1:
                        phrase += f", which is managed by {manager_clauses[0]}"
                    else:
                        mgrs = ", ".join(manager_clauses[:-1]) + f", and {manager_clauses[-1]}"
                        phrase += f", which are managed by {mgrs}"
            phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Join all predicate phrases for this subject into one clause
        subject_clause = join_parts(phrase_parts)
        sentences.append(subject_clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(sentences) + "."
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
